mutations.change: Decode BLOCKMAP origin words as unsigned values rather than signed little-endian 16-bit coordinates.
mutations.reason: The pinned and mini-map origins are negative and their exact world minima are independently frozen.

mutations.change: Interpret each BLOCKMAP cell list offset as a byte offset instead of a 16-bit word offset.
mutations.reason: The first list is addressed at word 868 and byte addressing cannot recover its framing or content.

mutations.change: Materialize the leading zero and trailing 0xffff framing words as linedef memberships.
mutations.reason: The independently decoded membership document excludes framing and validates every id against LINEDEFS.

mutations.change: Deduplicate cells by shared list offset and retain only one cell for each distinct BLOCKMAP list.
mutations.reason: All 864 spatial cells are required even though only 450 distinct source list offsets exist.

mutations.change: Sort or deduplicate each cell's linedef ids instead of preserving binary list ordinal and duplicates.
mutations.reason: Byte-for-byte round-trip and the ordered membership oracle make source list ordering observable.

mutations.change: Use truncation toward zero instead of mathematical floor when mapping negative coordinates to block cells.
mutations.reason: Probes immediately below negative 128-unit boundaries distinguish floor from truncation.

mutations.change: Read REJECT bits most-significant-bit first within each byte rather than least-significant-bit first.
mutations.reason: The hand-authored 0x92/0x49 mini fixture and pinned probes distinguish bit order.

mutations.change: Index REJECT as target-sector-major instead of source-sector-major row order.
mutations.reason: The full ordered relation is independently reconstructed using source times sector-count plus target.

mutations.change: Store only rejected pairs and omit explicit rows whose reject bit is clear.
mutations.reason: The fixed interface requires all sector pairs with a constrained zero-or-one value for direct negative filtering.

mutations.change: Create only right-to-left graph edges for eligible two-sided linedefs and omit their inverse directions.
mutations.reason: Every passable physical connection must be navigable and reachable in both directions.

mutations.change: Treat the 0xffff missing left sidedef as a sector and include one-sided walls as graph connections.
mutations.reason: The mini-map contains a deliberate one-sided line whose nonexistent endpoint must not become a vertex.

mutations.change: Include two-sided connections whose static vertical opening is zero or negative.
mutations.reason: The mini-map closed C-D boundary and pinned component counts expose non-passable height transitions.

mutations.change: Force sound_block to zero rather than deriving linedef flag bit 0x40 on both directions.
mutations.reason: The hand-authored B-C connection sets exactly that flag and expects it on both inverse edges.

mutations.change: Deduplicate graph edges by sector pair, losing distinct passable linedefs between the same sectors.
mutations.reason: Stable edge identity is derived from linedef id and direction, so parallel physical connections remain distinct.

mutations.change: Replace relational materialization with procedural byte-by-byte PL/SQL loops and dynamic statements.
mutations.reason: The database design requires set-based SQL and forbids a procedural shadow implementation or dynamic SQL.

mutations.change: Create only an ordinary edge view named like a graph and never create or query an Oracle SQL property graph.
mutations.reason: Catalog validation and GRAPH_TABLE execution require a real SQL property graph object.

mutations.change: Embed known cell lists, reject pairs, component counts, or expected hashes instead of decoding source relations.
mutations.reason: Source auditing rejects evaluator coupling and fixed expected outputs that cannot generalize to transformed mini-maps.
